mapping.b_element: conditions or constraints on the validity of the fluctuation-dissipation theorem

mapping.a_element: 1/f noise arising from suppressed zero-point fluctuations
mapping.b_element: fluctuation-dissipation relations linking noise and dissipation

brief_justification: While both papers address quantum fluctuations and their observable consequences, Paper A proposes a specific cutoff frequency for zero-point fluctuations to constrain dark energy, whereas Paper B critically examines the theoretical foundations of fluctuation-dissipation theorems and Langevin equations. The core mechanisms—empirical cutoff prediction vs. theoretical derivation of dissipation-fluctuation relations—do not align as the same underlying machinery.
